summary: British author of the Wolf Hall trilogy about Thomas Cromwell. Won Booker Prize twice (Wolf Hall, Bring Up the Bodies). Also wrote 'The Assassination of Margaret Thatcher.'

works.name: Wolf Hall Trilogy
works.description: Historical fiction: Wolf Hall (2009), Bring Up the Bodies (2012), The Mirror and the Light (2020).

examples: wrote about Thomas Cromwell in Wolf Hall

comprehensive_summary: Hilary Mantel wrote the Wolf Hall trilogy about Thomas Cromwell, winning the Booker Prize for both Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies. The trilogy opens with Cromwell being beaten by his blacksmith father Walter. Bring Up the Bodies follows Cromwell's scheme to bring down Anne Boleyn. The Mirror and the Light ends with Cromwell's beheading. Her story 'The Assassination of Margaret Thatcher' ends with 'Fucking rejoice.'

cards.front: Novel: Thomas Cromwell trilogy — Wolf Hall, Bring Up the Bodies, The Mirror and the Light; two Booker Prizes
Novel: Cromwell beaten as a child by blacksmith father Walter
Novel: loses family to sweating sickness
Novel: Cromwell schemes with Lady Rochford and Mark Smeaton to bring down Anne Boleyn, avenging Cardinal Wolsey
Short Story: IRA operative poses as boiler man Mr. Duggan; ends 'Fucking rejoice'
